title: Read books — wireless charging, economy, brain wave bs
html:
TL;DR

  • August 2019 idea-seed list: "Begin and advance ideas: wireless charging, economy, brain wave bs."
  • Three speculative-tech buckets a 16-year-old in GP is queueing for self-study — wireless power, macroeconomics, BCI/neurotech.
  • "Brain wave bs" is the earliest extant Yahir reference to brain-computer-interface territory; rhymes forward to the much later piercing prompts / mindless interface cluster.
  • Sits in the "go to the library and figure out what's real" phase that produced disruptive technologies at GPHS library five months earlier.
  • The dismissive "bs" tag is doing the 10x thinking hedge — I want to learn this AND I'm signaling I'm not naive about it.

Notes

Three-line note, scribbled on what looks like a "things to look up" afternoon. The trio — wireless charging, economy, brain wave bs — is a clean snapshot of the teen-Yahir disruption curiosity stack: an emerging consumer-tech (wireless power, then dominated by Qi), a knowledge-domain (the economy, undifferentiated), and a frontier-tech the teen is half-mocking (brain wave bs).

The "bs" qualifier is interesting: it's the same protective hedge that shows up in hide behind Bill Gates books — I want to be the kind of person who reads about this stuff, but I won't be naive about claiming it works yet. The note never names a specific book; the action verb is begin and advance ideas, which is the teen's manifesto cadence in miniature.

The brain-wave thread is the speculative-far-out one — eight years before piercing prompts and the mindless interface musings emerge in the 2026 corpus. The vault now has a documented through-line: teen-Yahir was already curious about the brain-as-input-channel before he had vocabulary for it.

Privacy note

None.

Connections

  • 2019 03 15 get disruptive technologies at gphs library — sibling self-study queue
  • disruption · 10x thinking
  • piercing prompts · mindless human interface disruption — far-future brain-interface descendants
  • grants pass teen era · grants pass or
  • scenario thinking — adjacent self-education-as-bet pattern
